assumptions: No prior Adobe Experience Platform account configuration or successful use exists.
The developer has the browser and command-line tools listed by the selected path.

developer_goal: Create or sign in to an Adobe Developer Console project for Experience Platform, obtain API credentials, and complete the documented XDM classes credential test.

first_success.outcome: An authenticated cURL request to the global XDM classes endpoint returns a successful response.
first_success.observable_signal: The XDM classes request returns HTTP 200 and a JSON response instead of an authentication error.

route_selection.surface: Experience Platform API auth tutorial + cURL XDM classes credential test
route_selection.rule: Prefer the official hosted quickstart and cURL when documented; stop at the first observable successful happy-path result.
route_selection.selected: Experience Platform API auth tutorial + cURL XDM classes credential test

candidates.name: Experience Platform API auth tutorial + cURL XDM classes credential test
candidates.reason: It is the specified official path to the first-success boundary.

required_path.action: Sign in to Adobe Developer Console and select the Experience Platform organization.
required_path.observable_result: The organization and an API project are accessible.

required_path.action: Create or open an Experience Platform API project and obtain the required credential values.
required_path.observable_result: API key, organization ID, access token, and sandbox name are available.

required_path.action: Set shell variables for the documented Experience Platform authentication headers.
required_path.observable_result: The terminal contains the credential values required by the API request.

required_path.action: Send the documented cURL credential test to the global XDM classes endpoint.
required_path.observable_result: The endpoint returns HTTP 200 with XDM classes JSON.

required_path.action: Inspect the response and confirm that XDM classes were returned.
required_path.observable_result: A successful authenticated XDM classes response is visible.

prerequisites.description: An Adobe Developer Console account with access to an Experience Platform organization.
Experience Platform API credentials, including API key, organization ID, access token, and sandbox name.

external_gates.description: The Adobe organization must be entitled to Experience Platform and the user must be able to create or use API credentials.
Access tokens expire and must be current when the cURL request is sent.

platform_outcomes.description: An authenticated cURL request to the global XDM classes endpoint returns a successful response.

excluded.reason: This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.

sources.title: Test access credentials
evidence_supported: An authenticated cURL request to the global XDM classes endpoint returns a successful response.
The XDM classes request returns HTTP 200 and a JSON response instead of an authentication error.

uncertainties.question: Does fresh-account UI wording differ from the documented Test access credentials path?
uncertainties.impact: A human should judge minor navigation differences without moving the first-success boundary.
